title: SEO Content Strategy
description: Research keywords, analyse search intent, and plan a 10-article content strategy designed to rank on Google and drive organic traffic.
whyItMatters: SEO is one of the highest-ROI marketing channels because organic traffic is essentially free once you rank. A well-researched content strategy can drive traffic for years after publishing.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of how search engines work

steps.title: Choose a niche
steps.description: Pick a business or topic area. The more specific, the easier to rank. Example: 'sustainable fashion in Australia' rather than just 'fashion'.

steps.title: Keyword research
steps.description: Use free tools (Google Search Console, Ubersuggest, AnswerThePublic) to find 30-50 relevant keywords. Record search volume, difficulty, and intent for each.
steps.tips: Long-tail keywords (3+ words) are easier to rank for and often convert better.

steps.title: Analyse search intent
steps.description: For each keyword, search it on Google. What type of content ranks? Informational (how-to), commercial (comparisons), transactional (buy now), or navigational? Match your content type to the intent.

steps.title: Competitive analysis
steps.description: For your top 10 keywords, analyse the first page results. How long are the articles? What topics do they cover? What's missing? Find gaps you can fill.

steps.title: Plan 10 articles
steps.description: Create a content plan with 10 articles. For each: target keyword, secondary keywords, search intent, working headline, outline (H2s), estimated word count, and content type.

steps.title: Write one article
steps.description: Choose your strongest topic and write a full article (1,500-2,500 words). Follow SEO best practices: keyword in title, H2s, meta description, internal/external links, image alt text.

steps.title: Create a publishing calendar
steps.description: Plan when each article will be published over 3 months. Include promotion plans for each.

deliverables: Keyword research spreadsheet (30-50 keywords with data)
One fully written and SEO-optimised article

rubric.description: Keywords are well-researched with volume, difficulty, and intent data.
Articles are planned to target a mix of difficulties and intents, not just high-volume keywords.
The written article is comprehensive, well-structured, and genuinely helpful.
The article follows on-page SEO best practices.
